description: A dramatic, high-contrast designed poster promoting ICE's 287(g) local law-enforcement partnership program, in vertical (portrait) format. Two officers are shown from behind, photographed (then duotone/desaturated and stylized) so their backs fill the frame: the left officer wears a ball cap and a tactical vest with white block lettering 'POLICE / ICE'; the right officer wears a tactical vest reading 'SHERIFF'. The background is a moody, cinematic glow of red-and-blue police-light coloring (red on the left, deep blue on the right with a bright bluish-white center burst), evoking emergency lights at night. Across the lower third in white type: 'Keep your community safer -' (lighter weight) above a large bold serif 'Partner with ICE'. The bottom-left bears the circular DHS seal and the 'U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement' wordmark; the bottom-right shows the URL 'ICE.gov/287g'. No suspects or arrestees are depicted. Messaging technique: heroic, solidarity-themed imagery (police + sheriff + ICE shown shoulder-to-shoulder) using cinematic lighting to make local-federal cooperation look authoritative and protective; the appeal is to local police/sheriff agencies and the public to support 287(g) partnerships. Intent is recruitment/persuasion to expand ICE's force-multiplier program.
notable_text: Keep your community safer - Partner with ICE. POLICE ICE / SHERIFF. ICE.gov/287g
